summary: Add self-start class to the external link button in EventHero so it sizes to its content instead of stretching to full parent width. The button sits inside a flex-col container which causes cross-axis stretch by default; adding self-start (align-self: flex-start) constrains the button to its intrinsic width.

fileChanges.description: Add self-start class to the external link button anchor element
fileChanges.details: Change className from "btn-primary mt-6 inline-flex" to "btn-primary mt-6 inline-flex self-start" on line 311. This prevents the button from stretching to the full width of the flex-col parent.

fileChanges.description: Add self-start class to the external link button anchor element
fileChanges.details: Change class from "btn-primary mt-6 inline-flex" to "btn-primary mt-6 inline-flex self-start" on line 179. Must stay in sync with the TSX editor version.

designDecisions: Using self-start (align-self: flex-start) rather than w-fit or w-auto because the button is a flex child inside a flex-col container. The cross-axis stretch is the root cause; self-start is the most semantically correct fix and works with both inline-block and inline-flex display values already on btn-primary.
